spec_match.rationale: Renders static and visitor-entered dual-presentation Unicode characters under all four font-variant-emoji values, including U+FE0E/U+FE0F selectors.

interactive.rationale: Live textarea and preset buttons update normal/text/emoji/unicode cells simultaneously, and the scenario focus controls remain interactive.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card now calls out the live editor and variation-selector use case, matching the page.

edge_cases.rationale: The live editor readout lists FE0E/FE0F codepoints and explains that `unicode` preserves selectors while `normal` lets platform defaults win.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, no static-card, columns use design system tokens.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP on localhost:3000 in Chrome 148 loaded /v131/css-font-variant-emoji/dual-presentation/, typed `Custom ☀︎ ☀️ ♥︎ ❤️ ✈︎ ✈️`, clicked all three presets, and clicked the six scenario-focus controls. The live cells updated together and computed to `normal`, `text`, `emoji`, and `unicode`; the readout listed FE0E/FE0F codepoints and explained unicode-vs-normal precedence. Console had no messages, network requests were all 200, and the feature conformance route reported 5 pass / 0 fail.
